item.title: Neuro-immune interactions underlying autoimmune diseases: insights from brain imaging data.

item.title: Toxicity of polycaprolactone nanoplastics, pristine or weathered in environmental conditions, to human intestinal epithelial cells, in vitro.
item.description: NanoImpact - Plastic, used in almost all everyday products, is a major source of pollution, particularly in the form of micro- and nanoplastics (MNPs). MNP impact on health, particularly on the digestive tract,...

item.title: Geographic isolation shapes the genetic landscape of the threatened karst-endemic plant Malania oleifera (Ximeniaceae).

item.title: Vitamin D3 as an immunomodulatory agent: molecular mechanisms, clinical translation, and precision therapeutic strategies.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Review - Vitamin D3 (VitD3) deficiency affects over one billion individuals globally, representing a critical modifiable risk factor for immune-mediated diseases. Beyond its classical role in calcium metabo...

item.title: A spectral component approach leveraging identity-by-descent graphs to address recent population structure in genomic analysis.

item.title: Ulcerative colitis, pathophysiological mechanisms and drug repurposing: a new therapeutic dawn-narrative review.

item.title: Association between oral microbiota and inflammatory bowel disease: A 2-sample Mendelian randomization study in East Asian populations.
item.description: Medicine - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD), is increasingly prevalent in East Asia. Its pathogenesis is linked to microbiota dysbiosis via the oral...

item.title: Mendelian randomization study finds no causal link between inflammatory bowel disease and cirrhosis.
item.description: Medicine - While some observational research has pointed to a connection between inflammatory bowel disease and increased cirrhosis risk, it has been difficult to determine if this is a genuine cause-and-effe...

item.title: Using autosomal and X-chromosomal SNPs to identify a victim of the 1956 Marcinelle mining disaster.

item.title: Properties of Runs of Homozygosity as a Measure of Identity by Descent.
item.description: Journal of animal breeding and genetics = Zeitschrift fur Tierzuchtung und Zuchtungsbiologie - Runs of Homozygosity (ROH) are commonly used to quantify autozygosity/identity-by-descent (IBD) in an individual or population.
